Rebel group MEND claims responsibility for sabotaging a major Shell oil pipeline in Nigeria.  
MEND alleges killing 11 government soldiers and rescuing two abused girls during the attack.  
Nigerian military confirms the pipeline attack but denies soldier casualties as "pure propaganda."  
Ongoing attacks have reduced Nigeria's oil output by 10%, impacting global oil prices.
c9414bf55859cb9ca7e925a4a09b416b6f66446a:
Top authors recommend their favorite summer reads for various moods and occasions.
Suggestions include one-day reads, long weekend books, and titles to savor all summer.
Selections range from classics and thrillers to poetry and childhood favorites.
Authors highlight both literary masterpieces and light, entertaining choices for every reader.
f12cda150b96acfa1499b2afb0a81029545abadf:
Barack Obama received key endorsements from former Clinton Labor Secretary Robert Reich and ex-Senators Sam Nunn and David Boren.  
Reich, a longtime Clinton ally, praised Obama's policy proposals as superior to Hillary Clinton's.  
Nunn and Boren will advise Obama's campaign on national security issues.  
The endorsements come just days before the crucial Pennsylvania primary.
7359741b0e60b4402f069963a26c7aa766f45e7c:
Researchers successfully reprogrammed human skin cells to act like embryonic stem cells, sparking debate on ethical research.  
President Bush and supporters claim the breakthrough validates restrictions on embryonic stem cell research funding.  
Opponents argue embryonic stem cell research remains essential for medical advancements and should continue alongside new methods.  
Scientists emphasize that progress in reprogramming cells relied on previous embryonic stem cell research.
b10c71f49dbeabad23325d625e8e7b8529429607:
Josef Fritzl vacationed in Thailand while keeping his daughter imprisoned in a cellar for 24 years.  
Fritzl confessed to raping his daughter and fathering seven children with her, DNA tests confirmed.  
Family members held an "astonishing" reunion after the case was uncovered and children were rescued.  
Authorities found no evidence Fritzl's wife knew about the crimes; past sexual assault conviction records are being investigated.
c5a1e04f5f84d406ffedfc2f312c314d1bc03c46:
Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah pledges cooperation in Lebanon's political process and national unity.
Lebanon elects Michel Sleiman as president after a six-month vacancy, following a Qatar-brokered agreement.
The new government structure gives Hezbollah-led opposition veto power in the Cabinet.
Nasrallah emphasizes healing national divisions and promises inclusive opposition representation in government.
8a5035d3527c4db4357dd1db9fb2712008dccea9:
Texas appeals court rules state had no right to remove FLDS children from ranch.  
Children in state custody likely to be returned to their parents if ruling stands.  
FLDS parents describe emotional trauma and stress experienced by children in foster care.  
Child Protective Services reviewing court decision and considering next legal steps.
3f33d4cb25cd121619ba99eeb02709b72716d622:
Consumer Reports found that cheaper cars can cost more to own long-term than pricier alternatives.  
Depreciation and fuel costs are the largest factors in total ownership expenses.  
Insurance, maintenance, and repairs also significantly impact overall costs, varying widely by model.  
Hybrids like the Toyota Prius often have lower five-year ownership costs despite higher sticker prices.
e6f451d6667f142a64818a9d05dbc49115e7cbf9:
Presidential candidates are often labeled "elitist" due to their elite education and wealth.
None of the current candidates truly represent "average Americans" in lifestyle or financial status.
Efforts by politicians to appear relatable are largely superficial and unconvincing.
Media figures criticizing elitism are often themselves part of the elite class.
39622c01625247af942a6439699ff529db1d54d0:
Carmen Salva leads Esperanza de Vida, a youth environmental group in northern Argentina.  
The group organizes weekly mountain cleanups, collecting up to 60 bags of trash each Saturday.  
Local children and parents participate, learning to respect and care for their environment.  
Their efforts have inspired broader community involvement and support from local government.
02927c88776bcc84fc883435a5037b4c8ca70e83:
Sixty-four measles cases reported in the U.S. this year, highest in seven years.
All but one case involved unvaccinated individuals, some too young for vaccination.
Most cases were imported from other countries, especially Europe and Israel.
CDC urges travelers to update immunizations to prevent further outbreaks.
3ee31d8987c07ba4ea9423d39c5e333525316958:
Amnesty International's annual report highlights global human rights abuses in China, the US, Russia, and other nations.
China faces criticism for repressing activists, minorities, and censoring media, especially ahead of the Olympics.
The US is scrutinized for Guantanamo detentions, use of the death penalty, and lack of fair trials.
Ongoing violence and rights violations in Iraq, Afghanistan, Zimbabwe, and Russia are also major concerns.
f8ddc6d36f378757d27a244529f8fe11672baa41:
Judge declares mistrial in retrial of six men accused of plotting terrorist acts with al Qaeda.  
Jury deliberated for 13 days without reaching a verdict, marking the second failed prosecution attempt.  
Defendants, known as the "Liberty City 7," allegedly targeted major U.S. landmarks in their plot.  
Unclear if government will pursue a third trial after two mistrials and one acquittal.
4e97228ceef1be54ab9151fc6759017e6a54a18d:
U.S. Border Patrol agent Luis Aguilar was killed by a suspected smuggler's vehicle near Yuma, Arizona.
Aguilar was deploying spike strips when struck; the suspects fled back into Mexico.
Authorities from both countries are cooperating to apprehend those responsible.
Homeland Security officials expressed outrage and pledged swift justice for Aguilar's death.
0e649ccabaf0e650e9757ee256c2749d37486ba6:
Colorado woman Rozita Swinton identified as a "person of interest" in Texas polygamist ranch raid calls.  
Swinton arrested in Colorado for unrelated false reporting charges; evidence links her to Texas case.  
Authorities suspect Swinton may have impersonated a 16-year-old abuse victim in calls prompting the raid.  
416 children were removed from the ranch; court hearings underway to determine their custody.
9eccfcc231c7c0d65cb0c333cb3d1731a5fe2f3f:
Denzel Washington urges leaders to address the crisis facing America's youth.
High dropout rates, rising obesity, and youth crime threaten the next generation.
Boys & Girls Clubs and similar organizations play a vital role in positive youth development.
Citizens are encouraged to volunteer, donate, and hold politicians accountable for children's futures.
ddf109b3de6022a4ecd62ee2cd31d2c2ac51035b:
Thousands protested China's human rights record and Tibet crackdown as the Olympic torch arrived in San Francisco.  
The torch relay faced disruptions in London and Paris, with arrests and security concerns.  
China condemned the protests, calling them actions by "Tibetan separatist forces" and defending the relay.  
Some Olympic officials suggested reconsidering future global torch relays due to ongoing public relations issues.
bd92eab23374d95ac4430e2e1a8bf7561c662309:
Frankie Dettori and chef Marco Pierre White co-founded Frankie's restaurants after struggling to find family-friendly dining options.  
Their restaurant chain now spans London, Shanghai, and Dubai, and they've released "Frankie Dettori's Italian Family Cookbook."  
Dettori shares his favorite childhood recipe, Parmigiana di Melanzane, a vegetarian dish reminiscent of Sunday lunches.  
The article includes a detailed recipe and preparation instructions for the classic Italian dish.
d84440effc748f9220c7dde068962e803f566bc7:
Middlesbrough release Fabio Rochemback, Gaizka Mendieta, and Dong Gook Lee from their squad.
Manager Gareth Southgate hopes to retain goalkeeper Mark Schwarzer, whose contract is expiring.
Brazilian Rivaldo reverses retirement decision, will play for AEK Athens next season.
Tottenham's Jamie O'Hara signs a new three-year contract; goalkeeper Radek Cerny joins QPR.
e2866f596c85b0191ce812260d23855c70a01192:
Birmingham City co-owner and managing director interviewed by police in a corruption probe.
Investigation focuses on payments to a football agent and two players from 2002-03.
No allegations of financial benefit to club directors; inquiry relates to PAYE and National Insurance.
Club fully cooperating with police; share trading temporarily suspended during investigation.
4ebd9195bc8c12272b6281a5a610a7b5fb49b8cd:
Jaguars, once thought extinct in the U.S., have been recently photographed in Arizona near the Mexico border.  
Conservationists fear the border fence will block jaguar migration and threaten their survival.  
Environmental groups are challenging Homeland Security's waivers of environmental laws for fence construction.  
Wildlife experts urge balancing national security with protection of endangered species like the jaguar.
4bd37aa7004cd1fdf1ccb506d2159a2cf26995e0:
Sudan and Chad presidents signed a non-aggression pact to halt cross-border hostilities.  
The agreement followed two days of talks in Senegal, facilitated by President Abdoulaye Wade.  
Both nations accuse each other of supporting rebel groups crossing their shared border.  
The UN will help ensure the agreement is implemented amid ongoing refugee and displacement crises.
29b87dc549b8c64b35517e684e79b1da6316bd88:
International arms dealer Viktor Bout charged with conspiring to sell weapons to Colombian rebel group FARC.
Bout allegedly agreed to provide missiles, rocket launchers, and drones to be used against Americans in Colombia.
U.S. authorities seek Bout's extradition from Thailand and have frozen his U.S. assets since 2004.
Federal indictment includes terrorism offenses, including conspiracy to kill U.S. nationals and support a terrorist organization.
d2f2fe0b8797b416bae71305c9d1c874e2fdd0cc:
Hillary Clinton's chief strategist Mark Penn resigns amid controversy over Colombia trade deal contacts.
Penn's PR firm met with Colombian officials despite Clinton's opposition to the trade pact.
Colombian government fired Penn's firm, citing disrespect after his apology.
Clinton campaign clarifies Penn's meeting was not on behalf of the campaign.
0269d39abbea3edadd225fa97d818f5a789b4c72:
Thousands protested China's human rights record and Tibet crackdown as the Olympic torch arrived in San Francisco.  
Security concerns led to the torch being kept at an undisclosed location and a torchbearer dropping out.  
Protests in London and Paris disrupted the torch relay, prompting IOC discussions about its future.  
China condemned the protests, calling them actions by "Tibetan separatist forces" and defending its Olympic stance.
394f20c9bee9bc8d0e6a65bac48de57bd9f4733d:
Skybus Airlines shuts down, becoming the third airline this week to cease operations.  
Rising jet fuel costs and a slowing economy cited as main reasons for closure.  
ATA and Aloha Airlines also announced shutdowns due to financial struggles and bankruptcy.  
Passengers with future Skybus reservations advised to seek refunds from credit card companies.
6e63c8b1ae01ab91b4832222edaf4bb461b6c568:
U.S. sailor arrested in Japan for robbery and murder of a taxi driver.
Incident adds to recent controversies involving U.S. military personnel in Japan.
U.S. ambassador emphasizes cooperation with Japanese authorities for justice.
Local resentment grows over crimes linked to U.S. military presence.
355875856932fd634aaf7c6b26155403aaf15904:
"Street Kings" follows Keanu Reeves as a rule-breaking LAPD detective entangled in corruption and betrayal.  
The film, directed by David Ayer, sticks closely to classic Cop Noir tropes with little originality.  
Forest Whitaker energizes the cast as Reeves' mentor, while Hugh Laurie plays the Internal Affairs investigator.  
Despite strong performances, the movie struggles to offer fresh insight into the genre's familiar themes.
999523913d281ab57977d79b644f3879ac4d2e37:
Texas authorities are investigating child safety at a polygamist sect's ranch led by Warren Jeffs.  
The 1,900-acre ranch near Eldorado, home to about 400 followers, has been sealed off by officials.  
Social workers and police entered the compound after receiving a referral, but no safety issues have been confirmed yet.  
The sect, known for polygamy and controversial practices, is cooperating with the ongoing investigation.
5f7424604ec11358dc6fdfa47732f90422949041:
Mikhail Gorbachev claims Georgia provoked the South Ossetia conflict, not Russia.
Gorbachev defends Russia's military response as necessary to stop violence in Tskhinvali.
Georgian President Saakashvili criticizes Gorbachev for supporting Russian actions and spreading "lies."
Gorbachev warns U.S. support for Georgia could harm U.S.-Russia relations and global stability.
fdb2b65b9cb99b7d52559ae647cae293cb5910d3:
At least 19 schoolgirls and two adults died in a dormitory fire near Kampala, Uganda.  
Authorities are investigating the cause, with reports of an explosion before the blaze.  
Conflicting accounts emerged about whether dormitory doors were locked during the fire.  
Forty-two girls survived the incident at Buddo Junior School in Mpigi.
f974fe1b3777ffc5756322c0bb3f31a9b23ec09a:
U.S. officials suspect Pakistan's ISI aided the July bombing of India's Embassy in Afghanistan.
Evidence includes intercepted communications between Pakistani intelligence and militants.
Pakistan strongly denies involvement, calling the allegations unfounded and malicious.
Afghanistan and India have previously accused Pakistan of supporting militants in the region.
2930e1a7ac24bce649bfbf5f5971e74515e38a0a:
Gloria Taylor, mother of Damilola Taylor, died of a suspected heart attack in London.
She and her husband founded the Damilola Taylor Trust to help underprivileged youth.
Damilola Taylor's 2000 murder led to national debate and eventual convictions in 2006.
Police are treating Gloria Taylor's death as non-suspicious; a post-mortem is pending.
f5f7571a69e96c1d288d939861b7216c2bdc0ae4:
Pope Benedict XVI arrives in the U.S. for a six-day visit, greeted by President Bush.
Security is heightened with 27 agencies involved and no specific threats reported.
The pope will address key issues including the church sex scandal and interfaith relations.
Major public events include Mass at Nationals Park and an address to the U.N. General Assembly.
5f02aa32bd1dc95e47355755398e31550b232f8a:
Eight Florida teens to be tried as adults for videotaped beating of a 16-year-old girl.  
Charges include kidnapping, battery, and witness tampering; possible life sentences.  
Attack was allegedly retaliation for online insults and intended for YouTube posting.  
Experts warn of rising trend in violent "shock videos" among teens for online attention.
9177e5ac94f038749e8d4eb526a65461e0f6df4c:
Judge found Heather Mills exaggerated her wealth and career success before marrying McCartney.  
Mills' claim of being McCartney's business partner was dismissed as "make-belief."  
McCartney's generosity acknowledged, but Mills not entitled to his valuable paintings.  
Court valued McCartney's fortune at £400 million, rejecting Mills' higher estimate.
f12e4bbb07211de7d43b4e331dc73404aa804562:
Presidential daughters have led fascinating and varied lives, often shaped by their fathers' legacies.
Sarah Knox Taylor Davis married Jefferson Davis but died young from malaria shortly after their wedding.
Elizabeth Harrison Walker became a pioneering lawyer and economic commentator for women in the early 20th century.
Margaret Truman Daniel transitioned from singer to prolific author, famously defended by her father, President Truman.
